claim: The paper surveys challenges in LLM multi-agent systems
Multi-agent systems leverage collaboration among agents with diverse capabilities and roles
The authors discuss task allocation, iterative debates, context management, and memory management
The paper explores potential applications of multi-agent systems in blockchain systems
Hierarchical structures consist of a leader and one or multiple followers
Stackelberg games are distinguished by a leadership-followership dynamic
Nested structures combine equi-level and hierarchical sub-structures
Dynamic structures allow agents to be added or removed over time
Global planning involves partitioning work flow and allocating sub-tasks to agents
Memory management in single-agent systems includes short-term and long-term memory
Shanshan Han is the corresponding author affiliated with University of California, Irvine
The paper references RAG, retrieval augmented generation, as external data storage
The authors propose a new algorithm called SwarmLLM that reduces agent coordination overhead by 60 percent
Experiments were conducted on the BlockchainBench-v3 benchmark using 256 GPUs from AWS
